families.name: Algebra Setup & Representation
families.summary: Choose or construct the right Ore algebra and generators.

families.name: Operator Algebra
families.summary: Compute operator relations such as gcrd, lclm, factors, local structure, and ideal bases.

families.name: Closure & Transformation
families.summary: Derive annihilators for transformed, combined, or converted operators.

families.name: D-Finite Objects & Sequences
families.summary: Work with D-finite functions or sequences at a user-facing level.

families.name: Analytic/Numerical Computation
families.summary: Numerically evaluate solutions or continue them along paths.

families.name: Guessing
families.summary: Guess recurrences or differential equations from sampled data.

families.name: Advanced
families.summary: Later-phase symbolic workflows such as telescoping, desingularization, or multivariate elimination.

workflows.title: Create Or Choose An Ore Algebra
workflows.summary: Build the right Ore algebra and generator naming before doing operator work.
prompt_rules: Match the generator prefix to the intended operator type before doing downstream work.
Keep base-ring variables and generator names consistent.

workflows.title: Create Or Choose A Multivariate Ore Algebra
workflows.summary: Build a multivariate Ore algebra with several generators before ideal or telescoping work.
prompt_rules: Declare every base-ring variable and Ore generator explicitly in the intended order.
For multivariate tasks, keep the algebra object visible before constructing operators or ideals.

workflows.title: Compute GCRD Or LCLM
workflows.summary: Find common right or left multiples/divisors of operators.
prompt_rules: Use exact symbolic operator methods and print the resulting operator.
Keep the task focused on divisor or common-multiple computation.

workflows.title: Find Right Factors Of A Recurrence Operator
workflows.summary: Find a right factor of a shift or q-shift recurrence operator.
prompt_rules: The cited right_factors implementation is for shift and q-shift recurrences and only searches first-order right factors.
When claiming a right factor F, structure code so the result can be checked as L = Q*F.

workflows.title: Analyze Local Behavior Near Singularities
workflows.summary: Compute indicial data and local basis expansions near a singularity.
prompt_rules: Return the specific local object requested: indicial polynomial, monomials, or expansions.
Keep the evaluation point explicit in the generated code.
If the notation is QQ(x)[y][Dy], construct the coefficient ring as R.<x> = QQ['x']; Q.<y> = Frac(R)[] (or R.fraction_field()[]), then build A.<Dy> = OreAlgebra(Q).
If the notation is QQ(x,y)[Dy], construct R.<x,y> = QQ[] and set Q = R.fraction_field() before A.<Dy> = OreAlgebra(Q).

workflows.title: Compute An Ore Left Ideal Or Groebner Basis
workflows.summary: Construct a multivariate Ore left ideal, Groebner basis, elimination, or related ideal invariant.
prompt_rules: Build the left ideal explicitly with A.ideal([...]) before calling Groebner, elimination, or dimension methods.
When the request is basis-oriented, print the resulting Groebner basis or elimination output rather than only intermediate ideals.

workflows.title: Derive Related Operators By Closure Combinatorics
workflows.summary: Build annihilators for sums, products, powers, associates, or polynomial combinations.
prompt_rules: Treat the task as an exact symbolic annihilator-construction problem.
Prefer the most direct closure operation instead of chaining multiple unrelated steps.

workflows.title: Convert Between Operator Algebras
workflows.summary: Compute annihilators under integral/composition or convert between differential and recurrence forms.
prompt_rules: Use the conversion method that matches the requested representation change.
For multi-step derivations, keep each transformed operator visible in stdout.
If the user asks for downstream coefficients or terms after conversion, continue with the recurrence-side computation instead of stopping after the converted operator.
For to_S conversions, build the source differential algebra with Dx and the target recurrence algebra with Sn.
When the question asks for Taylor coefficients or first terms after to_S, use to_list on the resulting recurrence operator with explicit initial values.

workflows.title: Evaluate A D-Finite Function Or Sequence
workflows.summary: Approximate or evaluate a D-finite object through its higher-level interface.
prompt_rules: Prefer the D-finite abstraction when the user asks about a function or sequence rather than a raw operator.
Print the final evaluated value explicitly.

workflows.title: Compute Sequence Terms From A Recurrence
workflows.summary: Generate first terms or isolated large terms from a recurrence and initial values.
prompt_rules: Use to_list for concrete first-N term requests.
Use forward_matrix_bsplit when the user wants isolated large-n or high-precision terms.
If the sequence terms come from an earlier conversion or guessing step, print the resulting list as the final output instead of stopping at the intermediate operator.
If the user also asks to infer or identify the recurrence, keep that recurrence visible in stdout before printing the term list.

workflows.title: Evaluate A Differential Solution Numerically
workflows.summary: Numerically evaluate a differential-operator solution at a point or along a path.
prompt_rules: Make the operator, initial conditions, and evaluation point or path explicit.
When a numeric answer is the goal, print the computed value clearly.

workflows.title: Compute Transition Matrices Or Follow Analytic Continuation Paths
workflows.summary: Continue solutions along a path or compute a transition matrix between local bases.
prompt_rules: Keep the path explicit and ordered in the code.
Treat path-dependent continuation as distinct from single-point evaluation.

workflows.title: Guess A Recurrence Or Differential Equation From Data
workflows.summary: Infer an operator from sequence terms or sampled coefficients.
prompt_rules: Make the data and target algebra explicit before guessing.
If a default guess call fails, narrow the search window instead of abandoning the task.
If the user asks to continue the sequence after guessing, use the guessed recurrence to print the requested terms.
When the question asks for both the inferred recurrence and predicted terms, print both the guessed operator and the resulting term list.

If the user asks to find an operator that annihilates a sequence, generate enough terms of the sequence as a list and use guess(data, A) to discover the annihilating operator. Prefer guess(data, A) over guess_rec(data, n, Sn) because guess_rec has coercion issues on some data.

workflows.title: Advanced Symbolic Workflow
workflows.summary: Handle later-phase workflows such as telescoping or desingularization.
prompt_rules: These workflows are expert-mode and should stay narrow and explicit.
If context is weak, say what additional evidence is needed.

workflows.title: Run Multivariate Creative Telescoping
workflows.summary: Compute telescopers, and optionally certificates, from a multivariate annihilating ideal.
prompt_rules: Start from the annihilating ideal in a multivariate Ore algebra, then apply ct or creative telescoping to the elimination variable.
If the user asks for certificates, keep the telescoper/certificate pair explicit in stdout.
